user_symptom: Increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on metrics extracted using log filters.

user_symptom_category: Increased error rates with CloudWatch APIs
user_symptom: We are investigating increased error rates with the CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics and GetMetricData APIs when requesting metrics older than 3 hours in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if the period of the alarm is longer than 3 hours.
Customers experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch metrics for load balancers in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Metrics PutMetricData and CloudWatch Logs PutLogEvents APIs in the AP-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state, and some metric data may be missing.

user_symptom_category: Elevated query error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights
user_symptom: Between March 3, 8:25 PM and March 4, 6:17 AM PST, some customers experienced increased delays for log events in query results in CloudWatch Logs Insights in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Between 7:01 AM and 4:20 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates and latencies for CloudWatch Logs APIs in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region. The issue has been resolved and the service is operating normally.

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

user_symptom_category: Instance Unavailability and Insufficient Data in EC2 Instance Status Checks
user_symptom: During this time, some customers also experienced insufficient-data in the results from EC2 instance status checks for instances that were not impacted by the power event.
Some customers experienced degraded performance for some EBS volumes in the US-EAST-2 Region.

user_symptom_category: Elevated API faults and alarm delays
user_symptom: Increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the EU-WEST-2 Region.

Some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log events. Metrics filtered from those log events were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics may have transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
Delays in delivering scheduled CloudWatch events and AWS Lambda functions not being invoked successfully.

user_symptom_category: Elevated API Errors and Delayed metrics
user_symptom: Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Increased faults and latencies for CloudWatch APIs and metrics in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Elevated API faults and some delayed metrics in EU-WEST-2 Region. Elevated API faults and latencies for the GetMetricData and GetMetricWidgetImage APIs in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Customers experienced elevated API fault rates and latencies in the EU-WEST-2 Region. Some metrics were also delayed initially.

Increased delays for CloudWatch metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on delayed metrics. Some customers may have experienced intermittent delays in the availability of CloudWatch ELB metrics in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

user_symptom_category: Increased API Error Rates and Latencies
user_symptom: We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies in the US-WEST-1 Region.
Increased connection errors and latencies for CloudWatch APIs. CloudWatch alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on delayed metrics.

Some customers experienced increased delays for CloudWatch log event processing for metric filter extraction and log subscriptions in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics extracted using Logs Filter Patterns were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on those delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.
We are investigating increased error rates and delays for CloudWatch Logs Insights queries in the US-EAST-2 Region.
Between 3:28 AM and 5:00 AM PDT customers experienced elevated errors listing metrics via the CloudWatch ListMetrics API and the CloudWatch Management Console in the US-WEST-2 Region.

Customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch Logs APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Log events and metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

Log events and metrics may be delayed. Alarms may transition into 'INSUFFICIENT_DATA' state if set on delayed metrics.

user_symptom: Customers experienced increased faults for CloudWatch alarms APIs and delays in processing some alarms in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Scheduled events not being delivered via CloudWatch Events in the US-WEST-2 Region. AWS Lambda functions invoked in response to CloudWatch events not getting invoked successfully.

Some customers experienced elevated faults when calling CloudWatch APIs in the EU-WEST-1 Region. Some metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

user_symptom_category: API Error Rates and Metric Delays
user_symptom: Some customers experienced elevated error rates when calling CloudWatch APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Metrics were delayed, and CloudWatch alarms on delayed metrics transitioned into the INSUFFICIENT_DATA state.

We are investigating increased provisioning latencies for new load balancers, delayed propagation of load balancer CloudWatch metrics, and inconsistent health check statuses from the DescribeTargetHealth API for Network Load Balancers in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Customers experienced elevated faults when making GetMetricStatistics API requests for metrics data more than two days old in the US-EAST-1 Region. CloudWatch Dashboards were unavailable. CloudWatch logs customers experienced elevated API faults and delayed log events.

user_symptom_category: Latency and inability to add contacts
user_symptom: We are investigating latency in the ability to search contacts, and the inability to add contacts to rooms and calls in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating increased API error rates in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.

user_symptom_category: Increased API Error Rates and Latency
user_symptom: We are investigating increased error rates and latency for the Identity, Registry, and Rules Engine APIs in the US-EAST-1 Region.

user_symptom_category: Increased API and Invoke Error Rates
user_symptom: We are investigating increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region. We can confirm increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region. Between 11:07 AM and 12:50 PM PST, we experienced increased invoke and API error rates in the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region. The issue has been resolved and the service is operating normally.
We continue to see increased error rates and latencies for invokes in the US-EAST-1 region.
We are investigating increased API error rates and latencies for the Amazon EC2 Container Service APIs in the US-WEST-2 Region.
Fargate task launches are currently experiencing increased insufficient capacity errors.
We are currently experiencing increased error rates in the GetRecommendations API in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Between 3:19 PM and 5:28 PM PST we experienced increased API error rates in AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.

Between 10:08 AM PST and 12:10 PM PST a subset of Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics applications were not processing data in US-EAST-1 Region.
Elevated error rates for the VPC Management Console in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.
Increased API error rates in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region between 9:00 AM and 9:18 AM PST, now resolved and service operating normally.

service_name: AWS services in the US-EAST-1 Region

user_symptom: Increased API error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region between 12:43 PM and 12:59 PM PST. S3 Event Notifications continues to process a backlog of events.
Increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs, elevated launch failures for newly launched instances in the US-WEST-2 Region.
Between 9:37 AM and 1:49 PM PST we experienced elevated failure rate for sample app and custom SDK generation and cloud logic API creation for all Mobile Hub projects.
Investigating increased API errors for API Gateway in the US-EAST-2 Region. Elevated API error rate restricted to control plane operations for API Gateway Version 2 API (WebSocket and HTTP APIs).
Errors affecting the Billing Console including the Dashboard and the Bills page.

Increased deployment error rates in the US-EAST-1 Region between 9:37 AM PST and 5:20 PM PST.
We are investigating elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

Increased error rates affecting a small number of customers running Amazon Athena queries in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Customers experienced elevated latency and error rates in response to IAM API requests, delays in describing recently created or modified IAM resources, and propagation delays for IAM API updates in specific regions.

user_symptom_category: Increased Error Rates for Bucket Operations
user_symptom: Customers experienced elevated errors and latency for a subset of API requests to create and manage Amazon S3 buckets between 7:54 AM and 9:18 AM PDT.
Affected customers experiencing issues when attempting SSO authentication for the AWS console and services.

Customers may have experienced some delayed alarms in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

Increased error rate for the SubscribeToShard API

We are investigating increased API error rates in the eu-central-1 Region. We have identified the root cause of the issue causing increased elevated error rates in the eu-central-1 Region and are actively working to resolve the issue. We are starting to see some recovery in API errors in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Regions and continue to work towards full recovery. Between 1:10 PM PDT and 5:26 PM PDT we experienced increased error rates in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region. The issue has been resolved and the service is operating normally.
Increased latencies while provisioning new ElastiCache nodes and elevated API error rates in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Increased API error rates and latencies when establishing new connections to AWS IoT Core in the EU-WEST-1 Region.

user_symptom_category: Increased invoke timeouts and elevated latencies
user_symptom: Increased invoke timeouts and elevated latencies for a small proportion of AWS Lambda functions in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Increased error rates for API invokes in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Newly launched EC2 instances or new ENI attachments may experience delay in establishing network connectivity within the affected Availability Zone.
ENI attachment times continue to take a low number of single digit minutes to establish full network connectivity within the affected Availability Zone.
Some instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-1 Region experienced power and network connectivity issues, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes.
We are investigating network connectivity issues for some instances and increased error rates and latencies for the EC2 APIs within the US-EAST-2 Region.
Customers experienced elevated DNS failures when querying Route 53 Resolver for certain hostnames in a single Availability Zone (use1-az4) in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are currently investigating degraded performance for some EBS volumes in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
We are investigating connectivity issues affecting some database instances in a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region.
experienced elevated packet loss to and from some destinations on the Internet in the SA-EAST-1 Region
some instances were unreachable and some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region
Increased error rates for (some) new launches in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region between July 21 11:45 PM and July 22 12:56 AM PDT.
Connectivity issues for some EC2 instances, increased API error rates, and degraded performance for some EBS volumes within a single Availability Zone in the EU-CENTRAL-1 Region.

Some instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the EU-WEST-2 Region. The affected instances did not lose power but became unreachable due to a loss of power to networking devices within the affected Availability Zone. Some EBS volumes experienced degraded performance during this time period and launches of new EC2 instances and the creation of new EBS volumes experienced increased error rates.

Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to the service. Gateways will continue to accept writes but will not be able to proceed to upload them to the service until the issue is resolved.
Increased error rates for instance launches in a single availability zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.
Between 12:30 AM and 7:10 AM PST we experience periods of network connectivity issues for NAT Gateway within the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
We can confirm Internet and EC2 Public IP address connectivity issues for newly launched EC2 instances within the US-GOV-EAST-1 Region.

A small percentage of EC2 servers in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region shutdown due to overheating. This resulted in impaired EC2 instances and degraded EBS volume performance for resources in the affected area of the Availability Zone.
Customers experienced elevated network packet loss that impacted connectivity to a subset of Internet destinations.
We experienced network connectivity issues affecting Direct Connect customers using the US-GOV-WEST-1 Region.
Some database instances in the AP-SOUTH-1 Region are experiencing degraded performance or are unavailable.
A small number of instances experienced connectivity issues in a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-2 Region.
Some customers experienced connectivity issues from the East Coast of the United States to AWS services.

user_symptom_category: Increased API and Launch Error Rates
user_symptom: Investigating increased API and launch error rates in a single Availability Zone in the US-EAST-1 Region.

Increased error rates for new launches, API error rates, connectivity issues for some instances in a single Availability Zone, elevated error rates for the RunInstances EC2 API, increased API error rates for the EC2 API, connectivity issues between EC2 instances and AWS services.
Meeting availability issues in the US-EAST-1 Region
Some RDS instances experienced connectivity issues within a single Availability Zone in the AP-NORTHEAST-1 Region.

Between 10:22 AM and 11:43 AM PST we experienced an Internet connectivity issue with an external provider outside of our network which affected traffic from some end-user networks and the AP-SOUTH-1 Region.
Storage Gateway VMs appear to be offline and are not able to perform out of cache reads or upload to the service.
Increased failures for newly launched instances within the US-EAST-2 Region. Changes to instance networking state, such as mapping Elastic IP addresses, may also experience increased error rates.

user_symptom_category: Networking Connectivity Issues, API Error Rates, Power Loss, Recovery
user_symptom: Increased EC2 launch failures and networking connectivity issues. Elevated RunInstance API error rates. Loss of power affecting availability and connectivity to EC2 instances. Recovery of EC2 instances and EBS volumes. Network connectivity issues slowing down recovery.
Customers experienced Internet connectivity issues for some networks to and from the US-EAST-2 Region. Some customers may be experiencing VPN connectivity issues due to this incident.
Customers experienced increased DNS query timeouts in a single Availability Zone (use1-az6) of the US-EAST-1 Region.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times to propagate certain configuration changes made either via console, CloudFront APIs, or AWS CloudFormation templates to some of our edge locations.

Increased propagation times of DNS edits to public and private Route 53 hosted zones, increased failures for various API and Console operations, impact on provisioning workflows for other AWS services using Route 53, errors for specific APIs like CreateHostedZone, DeleteHostedZone, AssociateVPCWithHostedZone, and DisassociateVPCFromHostedZone.
Customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to Route 53 DNS servers. Queries to existing DNS records are not affected by this issue and are being answered normally.
Delays in propagating changes to CloudFront distributions to edge locations due to the ACM issue in US-EAST-1.

user_symptom_category: Elevated error rates, high latency, longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.
user_symptom: We are investigating elevated error rates and elevated latency in multiple edge locations. We can confirm elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations, which is also contributing to longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. We are beginning to see recovery for the elevated error rates and high latency accessing content from multiple Edge Locations.
Route 53 Management Console was experiencing elevated error rates.
Between 5:14 PM and 7:09 PM PST we experienced elevated DNS resolution issues for some instances in the EU-WEST-1 Region.
Users experienced longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.
Longer than usual reporting update delays for change propagation of invalidations and CloudFront configurations. CloudFront Access Logs, Metrics, and Reporting may be affected.
Due to the DDoS attack against Route 53, some ISPs operating affected DNS resolvers implemented mitigation strategies causing DNS lookups for a small number of AWS names to fail.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations. We have confirmed that we are seeing increased propagation times for changes to a few CloudFront edge locations. Between January 29 9:12 PM and January 30 12:48 AM PST we experienced delays in propagation times for changes to CloudFront configurations.

Increased propagation times of DNS edits to public and private Route 53 hosted zones, increased failures for various API and Console operations related to hosted zones, and errors impacting provisioning workflows for other AWS services using Route 53 for DNS names.

We have identified the cause of the increased delays in validation and issuance for DNS validated domains in the US-EAST-1 Region and are working to mitigate the issue. Certificates can be requested but will be delayed in issuance.
We experienced SSL Handshake and BadRequest errors to some CloudFront edge locations.

user_symptom_category: Increased propagation times of DNS edits, affecting provisioning of new resources relying on Route 53 for DNS
user_symptom: Customers experienced delays propagating changes submitted to the Route 53 API, increased API error rates, affecting provisioning of new resources relying on Route 53 DNS
Increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers between 7:20 AM and 10:06 AM PDT.
Domain nameservers may unintentionally change for domains registered under specific extensions. Users are advised to re-apply nameserver settings using the Route 53 console.
We are investigating increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers.
Intermittent DNS errors resolving some CloudFront distributions in Japan.
Customers may have experienced longer than usual propagation times while making changes to CloudFront configurations.
Increased errors when making changes to some hosted zones, and creation of new hosted zones using the Route 53 API.
Customers might have experienced delays in reporting status change of CloudFront invalidations between 10:04 AM PDT and 12:20 PM PDT.

We are investigating longer than usual propagation times to propagate certain configuration changes to a few of our edge locations: the creation and deletion of CloudFront distributions and the updating of certificates for a CloudFront distribution.
We are currently investigating intermittent errors connecting to some custom non-S3 Origins from our Regional Edge Caches.

Customers experienced issues resolving newly created public EC2 instance names to private IPs within newly created VPCs in the US-EAST-1 Region. Creation of new Route 53 Resolver Endpoints was delayed during this time.
Increased propagation times of DNS edits to the Route 53 DNS servers

user_symptom_category: Slow propagation of DNS edits and throttling of ChangeResourceRecordSets API calls
user_symptom: Users experiencing slow propagation times for DNS edits and throttling of ChangeResourceRecordSets API calls.
